item.title: A Rising Star’s Abrupt Fall Shows Xi’s Crackdown on Overambitious Officials
item.description: Purges aimed at rooting out corruption and disloyalty have pushed many officials to focus on finding ways to please Beijing.

item.title: Aircraft Crashes Into Beijing’s Tallest Building
item.description: The rush-hour incident in a tightly controlled airspace came weeks after Beijing prohibited the flying of drones in the Chinese capital.

item.title: China’s January-May Industrial Profit Rose 18.8% on Year
item.description: China’s industrial enterprises expanded by 18.8% year-on-year for the January–May period.

item.title: China’s Assertions of Authority Over Foreign Ships Near Taiwan Draw U.S. Rebuke
item.description: China’s coast guard this month began issuing commands to foreign vessels on Taiwan’s Pacific side, an action that the U.S. decried as “destabilizing.”

item.title: China Sharpens Tools for Hitting Back at Foreign Sanctions
item.description: Beijing is preparing a new law that would add to its ability to punish foreign companies and individuals deemed to harm Chinese interests.

item.title: What to Know About How Iran Uses China’s Currency to Weaken U.S. Power
item.description: The takeaways from a WSJ investigation into Iran’s use of China’s financial system to evade sanctions.

item.title: How China Undercuts the U.S. in Iran
item.description: Beijing has built up the use of the yuan for the oil market and other international trade, giving Iran and Russia a way to evade Western sanctions

item.title: What the West Can Learn From China—Without Becoming It
item.description: Plus, how export controls can backfire unless allies move in lockstep.

item.title: Can You Pass the Test That Strikes Fear Into China’s High-Schoolers?
item.description: Try a selection of questions from the notoriously difficult “Gaokao” university entrance exam.

item.title: As China Encroaches, Even New Zealand Is Getting Serious About Its Military
item.description: Billions will be spent in the coming years—including on missiles, drones and base upgrades—as the remote country squares up to a widening threat.

item.title: The U.S. Navy’s New Insurance Policy for War With China Is an Australian Base
item.description: The Pentagon plans to deploy submarines to an expanding facility in allies’ strategy to deter Beijing.
